PER JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER MEAD, THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 13, 2018 AT 9 A.M. DEPARTMENT 302 DISCOVERY IN ROOM 301. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS TO PROVIDE A BILL OF PARTICULARS TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL BY OCTOBER 30, 2018. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS TO SUBMIT A REPLY BY NOVEMBER 6, 2018. IF BILL OF PARTICULARS IS SUFFICIENT, PARTIES SHALL ADVISE THE COURT THAT THE MOTION WILL BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE PRO TEM: ROGER MEAD; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT GLORIA LOPEZ'S MOTION TO PRECLUDE PLAINTIFF KAREN UCHIYAMA FROM GIVING EVIDENCE PURSUANT TO CCP 454 IS DENIED. THE COURT ADOPTS IN FULL THE RECOMMENDATION AND REASONS FOR THE RECOMMENDATION IN THE REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER MEAD. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT KAREN UCHIYAMA'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANT KAREN UCHIYAMA'S DEMURRER TO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS-COMPLAINANT GLORIA LOPEZ IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AMONG OTHER POSSIBLE REASONS, THE NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIMS ARE DEFICIENT BECAUSE THE DELAYED DISCOVERY ALLEGATION FAILS TO ALLEGE ANY FACTS SHOWING THAT MS. LOPEZ COULD NOT "THROUGH THE USE OF REASONABLE DILIGENCE" DISCOVERED THE FACTS ON WHICH THOSE CLAIM ARE BASED WITHIN THE ONE YEAR OF THEIR OCCURRENCE TO AVOID THE CLAIMS BEING TIME-BARRED BY CCP 340.6. AMONG OTHER POSSIBLE REASONS, THE FRAUD CLAIM IS DEFICIENT BECAUSE IT FAILS TO ALLEGE WHAT ACTS MS. LOPEZ TOOK IN RELIANCE ON THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS OR WHY SUCH RELIANCE WAS REASONABLE. IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH AND HER COUNSEL'S COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 128.7, MS. LOPEZ IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT CORRECTING THESE DEFICIENCIES. IF A FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS FILED AND MS. UCHIYAMA FILES ANOTHER DEMURRER, MS. UCHIYAMA IS REMINDED THAT A DEMURRER MUST SOLELY BE BASED ON THE WORDS OF THE CHALLENGED PLEADING AND JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE FACTS AND MUST NOT MAKE ANY ARGUMENTS BASED ON OTHER PURPORTED FACTS, AS THIS DEMURRER DID REPEATEDLY. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/HEK)
DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 63001852) FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT UCHIYAMA, KAREN Y DBA LAW OFFICES OF KAREN Y UCHIYAMA HEARING SET FOR MAR-20-2019 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

UCHIYAMA ON MS. LOPEZ'S CROSS-COMPLAINT ON SEPTEMBER 10, 2018 IS SET ASIDE. THE COURT CANNOT ENTERTAIN A DEMURRER BY A PARTY AS TO WHOM A DEFAULT HAS BEEN TAKEN UNLESS AND UNTIL THE DEFAULT IS SET ASIDE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185 =302/HK

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT KAREN UCHIYAMA's DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT for damages; negligence; breach of contract; fraud.

Cross-Defendant Karen Y. Uchiyama's demurrer to the amended cross-complaint is overruled. The demurrer was not timely filed, was not served in accordance with CCP 1005 and 1010, and Uchiyama failed to comply with the meet and confer requirements of CCP 430.41. The demurrer to the attorney malpractice (negligence) cause of action is overruled. Uchiyama cites the wrong statute of limitations as CCP 340.6 applies to the claim. Lopez sufficiently alleges discovery of the wrongdoing to render the cross-complaint timely. (Amended Cross-Complaint, 9-11; see, e.g., Favila v. Katten Muchin Rosenman LLP (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 189, 223-225 [where it "[did] not appear clearly and affirmatively from the allegations in the complaint" that derivative claims for legal malpractice and breach of fiduciary were time-barred because of delayed discovery, "[t]his issue must be resolved on summary judgment or at trial, not on a demurrer."].) The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is overruled. Uchiyama again cites the wrong statute of limitations because CCP 338(d) governs fraud claims. As noted, Lopez's discovery allegations are sufficient and Lopez alleges reliance with the required particularity because she continued to retain Uchiyama based on the misrepresentations. (Amended Cross-Complaint, 11.). The court overrules the demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action. Even if Lopez is not entitled to restitution, she still states a claim. Finally, Uchiyama has disregarded the Court's admonition in its prior order "that a demurrer must solely be based on the words of the challenged pleading and judicially noticeable facts and must not make any arguments based on other purported facts, as this demurrer did repeatedly."

Counsel for Lopez is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Karen Uchiyama, Esq., Telephone 415-563-9300, appearing on her own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT KAREN UCHIYAMA's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT

Cross-defendant Karen Uchiyama's demurrer to all three causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by cross-complainant Gloria Lopez is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Among other possible reasons, the negligence and breach of contract claims are deficient because the delayed discovery allegation fails to allege any facts showing that Ms. Lopez could not "through the use of reasonable diligence" discovered the facts on which those claim are based within the one year of their occurrence to avoid the claims being time-barred by CCP 340.6. Among other possible reasons, the fraud claim is deficient because it fails to allege what acts Ms. Lopez took in reliance on the alleged misrepresentations or why such reliance was reasonable. If she can do so in good faith and her counsel's compliance with CCP 128.7, Ms. Lopez is granted leave to file a first amended cross-complaint correcting these deficiencies. If a first amended cross-complaint is filed and Ms. Uchiyama files another demurrer, Ms. Uchiyama is reminded that a demurrer must solely be based on the words of the challenged pleading and judicially noticeable facts and must not make any arguments based on other purported facts, as this demurrer did repeatedly.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Karen Uchiyama, Esq. 415-563-9300 Cross-Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT KAREN UCHIYAMA's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT ON CROSS-COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendant Karen Uchiyama's motion to set aside the default entered against her on September 10, 2018 is granted. Ms. Uchiyama has made an ample showing of excusable neglect, surprise, mistake, and inadvertence warranting the setting aside of the default per CCP section 473(b). The default should never have been requested and, once entered, cross-complainant Gloria Lopez should have stipulated to it being vacated. Because Ms. Uchiyama had served a demurrer to the cross-complaint notwithstanding that it had been rejected for filing and Mr. Uchiyama had also served a notice of unavailability, counsel for cross-complainant Gloria Lopez had no reason to believe that Ms. Uchiyama was unable or unwilling to defend against the cross-complaint. Under these circumstances, the conduct of Ms. Lopez's counsel in seeking entry of default and refusing to stipulate to have it set aside falls below the standards of civility and professionalism expected of all counsel appearing in California courts. Ms. Uchiyama has until November 28, 2018 to file her demurrer to the cross-complaint.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)
